mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a strong and stable labor market, which supports robust consumer spending and corporate profitability. This fundamental economic strength translates into positive investor sentiment and drives S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative more than 55% of the time when UNRATE is below its 12-month moving average, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) crosses below a specific threshold, it signals a significant easing of monetary policy. This reduction in borrowing costs typically stimulates economic activity, makes equities more attractive relative to fixed income, and compresses discount rates, driving S&P 500 returns higher over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If, after FEDFUNDS drops below 4.0%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of subsequent instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales (RSAFS) and robust employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates powerful consumer demand and rising household income. This dual strength directly translates to higher corporate revenues and profits across various sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. Both conditions must be met for the signal to activate.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not statistically significantly positive (t-stat < 1.5) when both RSAFS and PAYEMS growth conditions are met, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a significant and sustained drop in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a deepening recessionary environment. This decline in manufacturing and mining output negatively impacts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to further declines in the S&P 500.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production is a key recession indicator
failure_condition: If, during periods of economic contraction, a significant drop in INDPRO is followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 30% of occurrences over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates growing consumer confidence and investment in real estate, which has a broad positive spillover effect on related industries like construction, finance, and retail. This overall boost to economic activity supports S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:housing market health impacts broader economy
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with HOUST when the trigger condition is met over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power for consumers and compresses corporate profit margins due to rising input costs. This environment often forces central banks to maintain tight monetary policy, negatively impacting equity valuations and leading to lower S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation typically hurts equity markets
failure_condition: If, when CPIAUCSL 12-month change is above 3.0%, the S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and unexpected increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a rapid deterioration in the labor market and broader economic conditions. This leads to a significant decline in consumer spending, reduced corporate earnings expectations, and heightened investor fear, driving S&P 500 returns sharply lower.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: If a sudden increase in UNRATE by 0.5pp or more is followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in over 25% of occurrences, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is slowing significantly while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains high, it indicates a stagflationary or recessionary environment. In this scenario, monetary policy is restrictive despite weak demand, severely pressuring corporate profits and leading to negative S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate prolonged economic weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:stagflation is detrimental to equity markets
failure_condition: If, under the specified conditions of low GDP growth and high FEDFUNDS, the S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 35% of observed instances over any 7-year period.
